seoTitle: How to Upload Bank & Credit Card PDF Statements to QBO
metaDescription: Learn how to upload bank and credit card statement PDF transactions into QuickBooks Online so you can bring in data without manual entry.
intro: When your bank doesn't offer a direct feed, uploading statement transactions manually keeps your books current. This guide covers how to bring PDF bank and credit card statement data into QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Gather your PDF statement
steps.body: Download the bank or credit card statement PDF you want to bring into QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Go to the upload transactions option
steps.body: Navigate to the bank feed or file upload area in QuickBooks Online where you can bring in outside transaction data.

steps.heading: Convert or format the file if needed
steps.body: Depending on the upload tool you're using, you may need to convert the PDF into a supported format like CSV before importing.

steps.heading: Map and import the transactions
steps.body: Match the columns to the correct fields and complete the import so the transactions appear in your bank feed.

faq.question: Can QuickBooks Online read PDF statements directly?
faq.answer: Not always, native uploads often expect CSV or QBO files, so you may need a converter tool for PDF statements.

faq.question: Will uploaded transactions duplicate my bank feed?
faq.answer: Uploading data covered by an existing bank feed can create duplicates, so check for overlap before importing.

faq.question: Can I undo an import if something goes wrong?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically delete an imported batch of transactions if the upload didn't go as expected.

faq.question: Do I need to categorize transactions after uploading?
faq.answer: Yes, uploaded transactions still need to be reviewed and categorized just like transactions from a connected bank feed.
